meta.title: Full Project Renovation: AI-Driven Agile Archaeology & Refactoring Plan

common.fold_doc_tree: 📂 Click to expand: Archaeology working directory (records/ + main/, Agent writes here)
common.fold_i18n_json: 🔍 View: i18n data types & script specification details
common.fold_i18n_steps: Full translation prep steps ({n} steps total)

issue_tag_legend.meaning: Component or page; requires Figma To-be
Flow acceptance cases; must be all green before merge

L1.tip: Compare flow.md § Scoped Files: every .ts/.tsx in scope has an assignment. AI auto-verifies; unassigned files = 0.

L2.tip: Flow seven sections, endpoint closure, BDD alignment, transform consolidation. AI produces gap report; all green to pass.

L3.tip: Randomly compare against source code to confirm archaeology content is correct, not just complete. Human sign-off; core flows recommend 5 items.

Four zones.tip: Flows, Endpoints, Static Pages, Global Constraints — survey freeze requires all Four zones 100% checked.

Refactor-first.tip: After Phase 2 PM alignment, marked as flows/pages ready to start immediately; Phase 3 only these enter the work pool.

Align later.tip: spec + Figma As-is must still be ready, but refactor round one does not touch them; update To-be with PM after other renovation wraps up.

As-is.tip: Figma canvas 100% matching production; hard prerequisite for Phase 2 PM alignment.

To-be.tip: After refactor kickoff, only Refactor-first items get incremental updates; UI Issues need corresponding block To-be before starting.

common.s9_layers_note: This "three-layer defense" refers to API data flow (Upstream → Contract → Runtime), distinct from archaeology verification L1/L2/L3.

hero.title: Full Project Renovation: AI-Driven Agile Archaeology & Refactoring Plan
hero.subtitle: Use AI archaeology to fill missing Specifications (steps, pages, flows), clarify API Schema from a frontend perspective and build stable Transformers, rebuild the Figma design system, and drive documented acceptance through an Agent Skills pipeline — systematically reducing design drift and technical debt.

s1.heading: Background & Current Pain Points

table_rows.impact: Business logic cannot be verified; high change risk
table_rows.cause: No standard documents to verify business logic; steps, pages, and flows are reverse-engineered entirely from code.
Many anti-patterns make certain framework bugs extremely hard to trace and debug.

Backend has no spec docs — response formats cannot be accurately determined from frontend code alone, plus extra guesswork added to temporarily handle malformed backend responses. This is the upstream root cause of inconsistent error formats and scattered transforms.
Inconsistent error response formats force the frontend to add messy handling logic for each variant.
The frontend created its own transform layer, but because it is scattered everywhere, original values are hard to find quickly during refactors.

table_rows.impact: UI restoration by guesswork; design iterations untraceable
table_rows.cause: Figma is not maintained in sync with the codebase; production and design files have long diverged.

s1.vision_heading: As-is Pain Points vs. To-be Vision
as_is_items: No spec docs to verify business logic
Code degradation and accumulated tech debt
Chaotic backend formats, scattered frontend transforms
to_be_items: All archaeologized business behavior has BDD acceptance cases
Figma design system synced with code
AI Agent automated dispatch + clear human-AI collaboration boundaries
Data boundary isolation; clean types replace dirty data

s2.heading: Top-Level Architecture, Doc Flow & Directory Spec
s2.subheading: Complete output chain from legacy codebase to survey freeze. Archaeology working data writes to records/ and main/; after survey completion, Docusaurus is produced separately for PM alignment.
s2.example_callout: ⚠️ `[flow-name]`, `[endpoint-name]` in this section and the example lists below illustrate directory structure and split granularity only — not real flows or APIs for this project. Actual names and boundaries are defined one by one during archaeology based on existing codebase business logic.
s2.taxonomy_intro: Flow is not the only classification axis. Endpoint specs are orthogonal to Flow — one flow can reference multiple endpoints, and one endpoint can be shared across flows.

taxonomy_rows.granularity: One complete user task (Entry → Terminal State)

taxonomy_rows.depth: Block structure + i18n keys only; no schema / BDD

s2.taxonomy_endpoint_note: Endpoint specs (docs/records/endpoints/) are another orthogonal axis: one endpoint per file; archaeology infers upstream structure; planner produces Frontend Interface + Transformer to main/contract-schemas/endpoints/.
s2.flow_granularity_heading: Flow split criteria (Highway Bus service — illustrative example)
flow_granularity_rules: Split by user task (User Journey), not by page or route.
One Record = one independently archivable complete journey (Entry Point → Terminal State).
Endpoint specs (endpoints/) and Flow (flows/) are orthogonal: one flow can reference multiple endpoints; endpoints can be shared across flows.

Independent Entry Point with complete journey → separate flow (e.g. Coupon flow via email deep link); logic triggered mid-journey in another flow (e.g. Coupon apply during booking) → write into that flow's Edge Cases.
Pages linking to external forms with no complete in-site task (e.g. Affiliate) → static page survey, no flow.
Cross-flow cross-cutting logic (session expiry, anti-abuse, URL locale sync) → consolidate to global-constraints/.
s2.flow_examples_note: Below illustrates split granularity types only; [flow-name] is a placeholder, not a real flow name for this project:

flow_examples.name: [search-flow]
flow_examples.desc: Search & filter (including status-driven hidden UI states)

flow_examples.name: [coupon-email-flow]
flow_examples.desc: Coupon flow via email deep link (independent Entry Point)

s2.endpoint_examples_note: Endpoint filenames below are examples; name per actual API during archaeology:
s2.diagram:
graph TD
    Code["Legacy source"] -->|Repomix bundle + Agent exploration| R1["🕵️ 1. Code Archaeologist"]
    R1 -->|As-is business behavior| Flow["docs/records/flows/[flow-name]/flow.md"]
    R1 -->|Infer endpoint response structure| Upstream["docs/records/endpoints/[endpoint-name].ts"]
    Flow --> R2["📐 2. Data Boundary Planner"]
    Upstream --> R2
    Comp["Components / Store derived logic"] --> R2
    R2 -->|Frontend Interface + Transformer| Schema["docs/main/contract-schemas/endpoints/"]
    R2 --> Ctx["CONTEXT.md"]
    Flow --> R3["✍️ 3. Behavior Test Architect"]
    Schema --> R3
    R3 -->|BDD cases| BDD["docs/records/flows/[flow-name]/e2e-cases.feature"]
    Flow --> R4["⚖️ 4. Documentation Auditor"]
    Schema --> R4
    BDD --> R4
    R4 -->|Coverage review| Check["docs/survey-checklist.md"]
    Check -->|100% Check| Freeze["Survey freeze"]
    Freeze -->|Organize survey docs| Docs["Docusaurus doc site"]
    Docs -->|PM alignment| PM["Business / technical change discussion"]
    Freeze -.->|Developer reference| Ref["records/ + main/ retained"]
s2.doc_tree_note: Below is the working directory for archaeology and refactor — for Agent writes and developer reference during refactor; not a PM-facing format.
s2.docusaurus_intro: After survey-checklist reaches 100%, organize survey docs (records/ + main/ + CONTEXT.md) into Docusaurus format, build a doc site in a separate repo as the formal interface for PM alignment. main/ and records/ remain for developer use and refactor reference.
docusaurus_steps: Use frozen survey docs as source (not writing Docusaurus during archaeology)
Skill / Script converts to Docusaurus structure (MDX, sidebar, Admonitions, etc.)
Consolidate each flow.md § Pending PM Clarification to Open Questions section
Deploy doc site in separate repo
Use this site for technical and business change discussions with PM

doc_tree_rows.desc: Global glossary (permanent). Continuously updated during archaeology; grill-with-docs reuses it.
Archaeology progress tracker. Four zones + L1/L2/L3 sub-checks per Record.
As-is archaeology workspace. Developer reference during refactor; retained after survey freeze, not PM-facing.
As-is current business flow. Name defined during archaeology from codebase (placeholder: [flow-name]).
Single endpoint raw response structure inferred from codebase during archaeology (TypeScript type). Name defined during archaeology.
Verified As-is flows; To-be SSOT foundation during refactor. Developer reference.

s3.heading: Producing Data via Skills Workflow
s3.subheading: Four roles with core tasks and precise I/O. Technical specs in collapsible drawers; Role 4 three-layer audit flow in dedicated section below.
s3.auditor_intro: Role 4 does not re-archaeologize — it verifies whether Roles 1–3 fulfilled template contracts. "No missing files" and "no missing details" are handled separately: L1/L2 can be auto-verified; L3 is human spot-check against source code.
s3.auditor_diagram:
flowchart TD Draft["① AI draft checklist"] --> L1["② L1 Coverage map
unassigned files = 0"] L1 --> L2["③ L2 Structure gate
template + cross-refs"] L2 --> Gap["④ gap report"] Gap --> L3["⑤ L3 Manual spot-check
compare source code"] L3 --> Check["⑥ survey-checklist check"] Check --> Freeze["Four zones 100% → Freeze"]
auditor_workflow_steps: AI drafts survey-checklist.md Four zones items from legacy file tree and existing artifacts.
AI runs L1: compare flow.md § Scoped Files, confirm every .ts/.tsx in scope has an assignment; unassigned files = 0.
AI runs L2: check flow seven sections complete, Referenced Endpoints closure (upstream .ts + contract-schema .md), BDD Scenarios aligned with flow.md, @old_logic_location appears in Scoped Files.
Produce gap report listing failures; if not all green, return to corresponding Role for fixes.
Human reviews gap report, performs L3 spot-check for that Record (see list below).
L1 + L2 + L3 all pass → check that Record; all Four zones checked → Phase 1 freeze.

auditor_layers_rows.checks: Whether every legacy file in scope has an assignment
flow template, endpoint closure, BDD alignment, transform consolidation
Randomly compare source code; confirm content is correct, not just complete
auditor_layers_rows.pass: all spot-check items pass (core flows recommend 5)

s3.auditor_spotcheck_heading: L3 manual spot-check list (per Flow Record)
auditor_spotcheck_items: Randomly pick 1 Edge Case → compare original component if-else, confirm flow.md description is correct.
Randomly pick 1 @old_logic_location → confirm transformer logic is consolidated, nothing missed.
Randomly pick 1 Unverified Logic → confirm truly cannot be explained from code (not a lazy tag).
(Core business flow extra) Pick 1 more Happy Path step + 1 Referenced Endpoint field semantics.
s3.checklist_intro: Different archaeology artifact types have different pass standards. Each checkbox must include L1/L2/L3 sub-checks to avoid "has docs but not verified".

checklist_rows.standard: flow.md (incl. Pending PM Clarification) + e2e-cases.feature + referenced endpoints all have contract-schemas; BDD aligned with flow.md
Corresponding main/contract-schemas/endpoints/[name].md exists and references upstream
main/pages/ has blocks + i18n key list (no BDD required)
global-constraints/e2e-cases.feature covers cross-cutting logic listed in s4

s3.checklist_example_note: Below is checklist entry format example (abstract illustration):

roles.title: Code Archaeologist
roles.task: Map the legacy system's current state (As-is): distill pure business behavior, infer related endpoint response structures and all possibilities from codebase, exclude dead code.

ai_recommendation.hint: Batch archaeology for large file sets

roles.spec_example_note: Abstract example below illustrates section structure only — not real flows or endpoints for this project.

spec_sections.title: ### Entry Points
spec_sections.desc: Which pages / routes / homepage blocks can enter this flow. Homepage spec only notes reference; behavior details written here.

spec_sections.title: ### Referenced Endpoints
spec_sections.desc: List of endpoints/[name].ts this flow involves, as index to the orthogonal endpoint axis.

spec_sections.title: ### Happy Path
spec_sections.desc: Minimal user success operation steps.

spec_sections.title: ### Edge Cases
spec_sections.desc: All business logic branches in code (excluding network outage and other infrastructure errors).

spec_sections.title: ### Pending PM Clarification
spec_sections.desc: Unclear business intent or scenario: code shows behavior but not "why" or correct rules. Note source location and question; after survey freeze, align with PM via Docusaurus Open Questions.

spec_sections.title: ### Unverified Logic
spec_sections.desc: Code mystery: branches written but never hit, cannot reproduce or explain. Note source file location; defer to production log verification or backend confirmation (not PM business questions).

spec_sections.title: ### Scoped Files (Coverage map)
spec_sections.desc: Required before Record archive. Every .ts/.tsx in scope must have assignment: referenced by flow section, Dead Code (with reason), Unverified Logic, or cross-reference to another flow. unassigned files = 0 before handing to Role 4.

roles.spec_code:
# [flow-name]

### Entry Points
- `/some-list-page` — User enters from list page option
- `/landing#quick-action` — Homepage block as shortcut entry to this flow

### Referenced Endpoints
- `endpoints/search-items.ts` — Query and filter
- `endpoints/submit-action.ts` — Submit main action

### Happy Path
1. User sets required conditions
2. System displays selectable results
3. User completes main action and sees success state

### Edge Cases
- Show empty state when no results found
- Block next step when conditions not met

### Pending PM Clarification
- Should certain identity types have different discount rules? Code has branch but no business explanation (`src/legacy/Pricing.tsx:120`)

### Unverified Logic
- `src/legacy/foo.ts:88` — Branch never triggered, reason unknown

### Scoped Files
| File | Assignment |
|---|---|
| src/pages/Booking.tsx | Happy Path + Edge Cases |
| src/utils/legacyCoupon.ts | Edge Cases: coupon expired |
| src/deprecated/oldFlow.ts | Dead Code (confirmed no route reference) |
| src/hooks/useMystery.ts | → Unverified Logic |

---
# endpoints/submit-action.ts (same Record output, abstract example)

/** @endpoint POST /api/some-action */
interface RawActionResponse {
  /** Field semantics pending confirmation; may not be literal meaning */
  status_code: string;
  payload: unknown;
}

roles.title: Data Boundary Planner
roles.task: Starting from archaeology upstream output, define To-be Frontend Interface and consolidate transform logic scattered in components and Store into Transformers. Does not touch live API directly.
input: Legacy component / store transform fragments to consolidate
roles.spec_example_note: Abstract example below — not real APIs or fields for this project.

spec_sections.title: Upstream Schema (references records/endpoints/)
spec_sections.desc: Use Role 1 .ts output as SSOT; note field semantic ambiguity and known backend quirks.

spec_sections.title: Target TypeScript Interface
spec_sections.desc: To-be clean frontend types. JSDoc notes @upstream_source and @old_logic_location.

spec_sections.title: Transformer Pure Functions
spec_sections.desc: Executable TypeScript transform function prototypes consolidating derived calculations from components / store.

roles.title: Behavior Test Architect
roles.task: Convert flow.md business logic into implementation-decoupled BDD cases, keeping traceable alignment with flow.md sections.
roles.spec_example_note: Abstract example below — not real scenarios for this project.

spec_sections.title: Gherkin syntax
spec_sections.desc: Must use Given-When-Then syntax.

spec_sections.title: Coverage aligned with flow.md
spec_sections.desc: At least one Scenario for Happy Path; at least one Scenario per Edge Case. Comment above each Scenario notes corresponding flow.md section.

spec_sections.title: Pending PM Clarification / Unverified Logic — no Scenarios
spec_sections.desc: Business pending items and code mysteries are not written as Scenarios; list separately in feature file top comments, referencing flow.md sections.

spec_sections.title: Exclude infrastructure errors
spec_sections.desc: 404/500/network outage handled by global Client — not written in Feature file.

roles.spec_code:
# Pending PM Clarification (references flow.md § Pending PM Clarification):
# - Certain identity discount rules pending confirmation

# Pending log verification (references flow.md § Unverified Logic):
# - src/legacy/foo.ts:88 — Branch never triggered

# Corresponds to flow.md § Happy Path
Scenario: User completes main action
  Given user has set valid conditions
  When user submits main action
  Then screen should show success state

# Corresponds to flow.md § Edge Cases: no results
Scenario: Query conditions yield no matches
  Given user has set valid query conditions
  When system returns empty result set
  Then screen should show empty state message

roles.title: Documentation Auditor
roles.task: Does not re-archaeologize — verifies Roles 1–3 fulfilled template contracts: AI runs L1 Coverage map and L2 Structure gate producing gap report; human performs L3 spot-check sign-off then updates survey-checklist.md.
output: Four zones + L1/L2/L3 sub-checks per Record

ai_recommendation.hint: L1/L2 auto-verify; L3 human spot-check sign-off

spec_sections.title: L1 Coverage map
spec_sections.desc: Compare flow.md § Scoped Files: every .ts/.tsx in scope falls into one of four buckets: referenced by flow / Dead Code / Unverified / cross-reference to another flow. unassigned = 0.

spec_sections.title: L2 Structure gate — flow.md
spec_sections.desc: Seven sections complete: Entry Points, Referenced Endpoints, Happy Path, Edge Cases, Pending PM Clarification, Unverified Logic, Scoped Files.

spec_sections.title: L2 Structure gate — endpoint closure
spec_sections.desc: Every Referenced Endpoint has records/endpoints/*.ts + main/contract-schemas/endpoints/*.md.

spec_sections.title: L2 Structure gate — BDD alignment
spec_sections.desc: Scenario count ≥ 1 (Happy Path) + Edge Case count; each Scenario comment has corresponding flow.md section.

spec_sections.title: L2 Structure gate — transform consolidation
spec_sections.desc: Files pointed to by contract-schema @old_logic_location must appear in Scoped Files.

spec_sections.title: L3 Manual spot-check
spec_sections.desc: See "L3 manual spot-check list" below. Static pages need L1 only; Endpoints need L2 only.

roles.spec_code:
## Flows
- [ ] booking-flow
  - [ ] L1 Coverage map: 0 unassigned files
  - [ ] L2 Structure gate: 5/5 passed
  - [ ] L3 spot-check: 3/3 passed (YYYY-MM-DD by @reviewer)

## Endpoints
- [x] post-reservation.ts — L2 passed

## Static Pages
- [x] /faq — L1 passed

## Global Constraints
- [ ] session-expiry — L2 + L3 pending

s4.heading: Core Archaeology Survey Flow & Execution SUPPLEMENT
s4.subheading: Priority and survey depth for three archaeology artifact types. Business uncertainties marked during archaeology; after survey freeze, align with PM via Docusaurus.
s4.example_callout: ⚠️ "Known examples" below reflect current project understanding; actual flow list and naming defined during archaeology from codebase.
pm_clarification_items: Archaeology phase: code mysteries → flow.md § Unverified Logic; unclear business intent / scenario → flow.md § Pending PM Clarification
Survey freeze → Docusaurus: consolidate all "Pending PM Clarification" to Open Questions section
After PM alignment: approved items written to main/ To-be; still uncertain items left as TODO + production log

table_rows.scope: Cross-page flows with complete User Journey (defined from codebase during archaeology). Known examples: main booking flow, search & filter, Coupon flow via email deep link.
table_rows.standard: Must complete four-role pipeline + Role 4 three-layer audit. Produce flow.md + contract-schemas + BDD; mark business uncertainties as "Pending PM Clarification".

table_rows.scope: Nav pages, FAQ, Top Page blocks, etc. (no CMS, stored in frontend translation files). Affiliate intro pages (link to external forms, no complete in-site task) also belong here.
table_rows.standard: No schemas or BDD. List page blocks and i18n keys in docs/main/pages/.

table_rows.scope: Auth state validity period (session lifecycle), error attempt limits (anti-abuse prompts), global state & URL sync (locale/currency refresh in URL)
table_rows.standard: Cross-page cross-cutting logic. Consolidate to docs/records/global-constraints/e2e-cases.feature; must run Role 4 L2 + L3.

s5.heading: Change Management & Scope Freeze
s5.subheading: Two gates: survey freeze (technically complete) → Scope freeze (PM decisions). After confirming spec and Figma with PM, start renovation from blocks "temporarily not changing".
s5.freeze_callout: checklist 100% → survey freeze → produce Docusaurus. Bring spec + Figma to PM for Scope freeze: which blocks are Refactor-first, which Align later. Start work from parts PM confirms will not change for now.

two_gates_rows.trigger: PM completes per-block decisions on spec + Figma
two_gates_rows.output: Each flow / page marked Refactor-first or Align later

phase1_items: records/ records As-is; main/ accumulates To-be contract-schemas.
flow.md marks "Pending PM Clarification" and Unverified Logic.
checklist reaches 100% → survey freeze, organize Docusaurus (incl. Open Questions).
phase2_items: Bring Docusaurus spec and Figma As-is drafts (see s6) to PM for per-block confirmation — including Align later items; both are required.
Discuss: what keeps current logic, what to refactor, what needs joint process rethink.
Close Open Questions one by one, or escalate to Scope decisions.
Mark Scope for each flow / page (see below).
s5.scope_decisions_intro: Core output of PM alignment. Decisions recorded as Docusaurus page badges and written back to main/ file frontmatter (scope: stable | pending).

scope_decisions_rows.label: Refactor-first
scope_decisions_rows.meaning: PM confirms this block will not change business logic or flow for now

scope_decisions_rows.label: Align later
scope_decisions_rows.meaning: PM indicates this block will be adjusted, refactored, or rethought later
scope_decisions_rows.next: Round one still needs spec + Figma As-is for PM decision; defer kickoff and To-be design. After Refactor-first completes, round two brings updated spec + Figma to discuss with PM

phase3_items: Start development from blocks marked Refactor-first; do not touch Align later items.
Align later items: after other renovation wraps up, re-discuss with PM and update spec + Figma.
Feature Branch modifies docs/main/ (To-be); PR includes CHANGELOG.md (Ticket Link + changed docs).
Docs merge with code on deploy — code is docs, docs is code.

s6.heading: Figma Design System & Three-Layer Token Architecture
s6.subheading: Serves s5 PM alignment: spec and Figma As-is appear as a pair. No Figma changes during archaeology; build As-is canvas after survey freeze; only Refactor-first items update To-be after Scope freeze.
timeline_steps: ① Archaeology: frontend does not touch Figma. Optional — ask designers to sync production site with new Token architecture (As-is restoration) in parallel with code archaeology.
② After survey freeze: build / complete Figma As-is canvas per spec (100% matching production), parallel with Docusaurus output.
③ PM alignment: all flows / pages (including Align later) need spec + Figma As-is together for PM confirmation.
④ Refactor kickoff: only Refactor-first items update Figma To-be; Align later stays As-is, changed after round-two PM discussion.
s6.pm_deliverable_callout: Minimum PM alignment deliverable: key screens per flow + static page blocks; spec (Docusaurus) and Figma As-is are both required.
s6.kit_callout: Figma starting point: use Obra shadcn/ui kit as community template (https://obra.studio/work/figma-shadcn-ui-kit/), copy to each project locally then override Tokens and As-is canvas — do not build component library from scratch. Code side pairs with shadcn/ui CLI.
s6.intro_p1: Multi-product "island maintenance": Figma Professional cannot link Variables across files — adopt "central template (Obra kit + shadcn components), local customization" — copy template, map brand colors, restore As-is canvas.
s6.intro_p2: Three Token layers align with Obra / shadcn philosophy: bottom Raw uses Tailwind palette naming; Brand builds 100–900 scales; Semantic aligns with shadcn and consolidates business-specific colors. Canvas binds only Semantic.
s6.pro_warning: ⚠️ Creating Figma Variables requires at least Pro plan Full seat (not Collab seat).
s6.token_rule: Three-layer Token physical isolation: canvas must and may only use Semantic Tokens. Raw (Tailwind palette) and Brand (100–900 scales) are forbidden on canvas.

token_rows.figma: Same names as Tailwind CSS palette (e.g. slate-500, blue-600, neutral-100)
Site brand colors 100–900 scales (e.g. brand-blue-100, brand-orange-500)
token_rows.css: theme defines brand scale; exposed on canvas via Semantic alias (e.g. --brand-pink)

token_rows.figma: shadcn standard (background, primary, muted…) + business semantics (e.g. link, brand-pink)
token_rows.access: 🔓 Only legal layer. Bind colors from Raw or Brand.

token_semantic_rules: Prefer shadcn built-in semantics (background, foreground, primary, muted, destructive…).
Semantic color sources: select from Raw (Tailwind palette) or Brand (100–900) and create alias.
Brand / business colors not covered by shadcn: name at Semantic layer (e.g. link, brand-pink), add CSS variable in globals.css :root pointing to Brand or Raw.
Figma Semantic Variables 1:1 aligned with :root variable names (follow shadcn/ui CLI conventions).
s6.fold_org_content: Central Workspace → Obra shadcn/ui kit template (Raw Tailwind palette + shadcn standard components). Each project locally → copy kit, override Brand scales and Semantic mapping, 100% restore As-is screens.
s6.fold_pattern_central: Central maintenance: 100% business-logic visual consistency, zero customization possible (e.g. Japan map selector) → Copy-Paste.
s6.fold_pattern_local: Local maintenance: composite components whose semantics change by product line (e.g. BookingCard, CouponSelector) → central shadcn base + local assembly.
s6.fold_align_desc: Figma canvas shows only human-readable English copy (company site primary locale is EN) — no i18n keys, no Variables Mode for translations.
fold_align_rules: Description can hold design notes (not keys): dynamic copy (API response), placeholder / char limits, truncation risk, PM copy pending, or link to Docusaurus spec section.
Sticky / Comment for PM alignment discussion only — not long-term SSOT.

i18n keys and multilingual mapping: legacy archaeology → JSON → Google Sheet → code (see s7); static pages also list blocks + keys in docs/main/pages/. During refactor, cross-reference via screen + spec / Sheet, not reverse-lookup keys from Figma.
s6.fold_align_example: Canvas: "Please enter your booking code" → Description: "Booking code placeholder; static copy; see booking flow spec §2" — not booking.field.code_placeholder.

s7.heading: i18n Fully Automated Pipeline
s7.figma_boundary: Figma shows only human-readable English copy (primary locale EN), not i18n SSOT. Keys and translations follow archaeology JSON → Sheet → code; design Description holds design semantics only (see s6).
s7.sheet_ssot: Legacy translations for all locales already exist during archaeology — cleanup only does format normalization, namespace / key rename, status tagging; no re-translation. When writing to Sheet, en / ja / zh_TW columns should already be filled; Sheet is the complete reference table.
s7.toolchain_note: Toolchain: Claude Web for archaeology JSON cleanup, Cursor for renovation. Target framework i18next → next-intl, format adjusted per next-intl conventions; status tags unused / in-progress.
s7.gemini_rule: Gemini is not required for archaeology: Sheet already has all legacy locales. Only when refactor adds keys (in-progress) or manual translation delegation, use Gemini to operate Sheet; Instruction Sheet holds Tone Guide for supplementary translation.
s7.iron_rule: System Prompt-level global rule: if needed translation does not exist in JSON, NEVER invent new keys. Hardcode in code with // TODO: i18n-missing for batch extraction.
normalize_rules: Framework migration: reorganize from i18next structure to next-intl conventions (namespace + key, JSON / message format per next-intl sync script output).
Do not actively strip extra whitespace — legacy may preserve it intentionally for layout; carry through as-is during cleanup.

Newline merge: legacy renders array [a, b, c] sentences via map → consolidate to single key with 
 newlines (e.g. "Line one
Line two
Line three") for easier translator editing in one column.

Interpolation syntax: during archaeology cleanup, i18next {{var}} directly becomes next-intl {var}; written to Sheet as final form; sync script only reads/writes, no format conversion — for future reuse.
Code side: copy with \n rendered via CSS whitespace-pre-line (or equivalent), no longer map array sentences in components.
namespace_rules: Single-domain project: namespace per page / screen, no -page suffix (e.g. checkout, affiliate-intro).
Cross-page shared copy → common; each page references only its namespace + common. Flow does not get its own namespace — each step screen uses its page's translations.
Slug aligns with docs/main/pages/, Figma frame names, and route Entry screen; legacy_key retains old name for comparison during cleanup.
s7.fold_json_content:
JSON format example: status, namespace, key, legacy_key, en, ja, zh_TW, description.

Locale values come from legacy translations (not empty shells awaiting translation). Example: namespace checkout + key coupon.btn.apply; legacy_key for script gap detection.
sync_rules: Split files by namespace: messages/{locale}/{namespace}.json (e.g. messages/en/common.json, messages/en/checkout.json).
Nested JSON inside files (e.g. { "coupon": { "btn": { "apply": "…" } } }). Sheet key column still uses dot notation (coupon.btn.apply) for editing; sync script expands to nested on write.
Sheet namespace column 1:1 with filename; pages use useTranslations('{namespace}') + t('coupon.btn.apply'); common shared across pages.
steps: AI organizes legacy translations to JSON (i18next → next-intl; {{var}} → {var}; array newlines merged to \n; no trim of extra whitespace)
Script compares legacy_key with original translations, checks gaps (dynamic keys manually marked active)
Script writes to Google Spreadsheet (single sheet + namespace column; all locale columns filled)
New project sync script: Sheet → messages/{locale}/{namespace}.json (dot key to nested JSON; unused filtered, in-progress shows 🚧)
SKILL.md rule: missing keys forbidden — hardcode + // TODO: i18n-missing; new keys marked in-progress then translated

s8.heading: Survey, Validation & Renovation Kickoff
validation_items: Legacy and new projects in same workspace for side-by-side comparison.
Multi-line copy with \n: frontend uses CSS whitespace-pre-line (see s7 newline merge rules), no longer map array sentences.
After survey freeze, organize records/ + main/ survey docs into Docusaurus format, deploy separate repo, align with PM (working directory itself not PM-facing).
gate_project_items: Figma As-is 100% ready (completed parallel with Docusaurus after survey freeze; prerequisite for Scope freeze)
Scope freeze complete: each flow / page marked Refactor-first or Align later
Align later items still need spec + Figma As-is in round one, but do not enter work pool

gate_issue_rows.requirement: flow.md + main/contract-schemas/ spec sufficient → TDD kickoff
Above + Figma To-be for that block (Refactor-first items)
Happy Path Scenarios written; Pending PM Clarification / Unverified Logic stay as comments, no Scenarios
docs/main/pages/ blocks + i18n key list sufficient; BDD not required

gate_merge_items: Slice BDD all green (flows with written Scenarios)
AI L1/L2 structure audit + human L3 spot-check (see s3)
PM sign-off on that Refactor-first block (Docusaurus spec + Figma To-be comparison)
s8.log_rule: During refactor, logic behavior must align with legacy. Branches listed in flow.md § Unverified Logic must add runtime log in refactor version to observe if still triggered — silent deletion or skipping forbidden.
log_rule_items: Unverified Logic marked during archaeology: keep equivalent branch and add log (reference flow.md file location).
After observation period with zero hits → can delete branch and update flow.md § Scoped Files.
New unexplained branches found during refactor: first tag flow.md § Unverified Logic then add log; do not remove directly.
s8.skills_heading: Issue splitting & Matt Pocock Agent Skills
s8.skills_subheading: Split Issues using 5 Agent Skills. Leverage monorepo integration. Parallel Issue tasks.

skills_rows.name: Grill-with-Docs
skills_rows.desc: Combine archaeology docs, clarify decision branches along design tree

skills_rows.name: To-PRD
skills_rows.desc: Produce PRD from consensus with User Stories and module sketches

skills_rows.name: To-Issues
skills_rows.desc: Split PRD into vertical-slice Issues with blocking relationships

skills_rows.name: TDD
skills_rows.desc: Red-green-refactor loop — one test one implementation, no feature loss on refactor

skills_rows.name: Architecture-Refactor
skills_rows.desc: Deepen shallow modules, clarify test boundaries for stable Agent navigation

issues.title: Implement transformBookingAmount pure function
issues.blocks: Refactor-first + flow.md / schema ready → can start

issues.title: Implement BookingCard static component per Figma To-be
issues.blocks: Blocked by #01; needs To-be draft for that block

issues.title: Write booking code validation Edge Cases acceptance cases
issues.blocks: Parallel with #01; BDD must be green before merge

roadmap.title: Phase 1: Archaeology & survey freeze
roadmap.desc: Four-role Skills → records/ + main/ → checklist 100% (survey freeze). Then parallel Docusaurus and Figma As-is (s6 ②, 100% matching production) — both ready before Phase 2. Optional kit sync during archaeology ①.

roadmap.title: Phase 2: Scope freeze
roadmap.desc: Prerequisites met: Docusaurus spec + Figma As-is both ready. PM confirms each flow / page as pair, marks Refactor-first / Align later (s6 ③).
roadmap.risk: 🔥 Risk control: cannot enter PM alignment until Figma As-is complete

roadmap.title: Phase 3: Refactor kickoff
roadmap.desc: After project-level + Issue thresholds pass, only Refactor-first vertical slices start; Figma To-be incrementally updated with UI Issues (s6 ④). Pre-merge BDD + L3 + PM sign-off.

s8.roadmap_note: Figma As-is is not done at refactor time — it is a hard prerequisite for Phase 2 PM alignment, completed parallel with Docusaurus after survey freeze.

s9.heading: API Data Boundary: No BFF, Zod + Transformers
s9.subheading: Conclusion after reviewing "Frontend-Backend Responsibility Split & BFF Design": no separate BFF; frontend fetches Core API then uses Zod for runtime validation and data normalization (Parse, don't validate). Aligns with contract-schemas produced by s3 Data Boundary Planner.
s9.decision_callout: No BFF layer — less infra and deployment cost. Frontend `httpClient` connects directly to backend; client-side Zod Schema + Transformer consolidates legacy odd formats; components only consume Clean Type.

layers_rows.role: Role 1 infers backend Raw structure from codebase
Role 2 defines Frontend Interface + Transformer prototype
refactor implements Zod parse + transform + handleResponse

runtime_steps: httpClient fetch Core API → get unknown
Zod Backend*Schema.safeParse — intercept HTML / non-JSON / field drift (ApiProtocolError)
.transform() to frontend Clean Type (snake_case, semantic fields, strip redundancy)
Unified ApiResponseSuccess / ApiResponseError envelope — business errors throw ApiBusinessError
React / Store only import Clean Type; direct Raw JSON forbidden

error_rows.trigger: Non-JSON, HTML error page, Zod parse failure
error_rows.handling: log + unified error UI; addresses s1 "inconsistent error formats" root cause
Branch by code; no more guesswork in components

s9.proto_intro: Previously implemented multiple endpoints per above pattern (get-bus-list, get-locations, submit-contact…), each with schema.ts (Backend + Clean), transformer.ts, index.ts, shared handleResponse and contract.ts.
proto_pause_items: As endpoint count grows, easy to get lost between folders — missing naming reference SSOT with flows / archaeology artifacts
Backend naming (PascalCase, nested XML style) hard to align with frontend Clean naming across APIs
shared entities (e.g. BusFacilities) vs. per-endpoint boundaries unclear; duplicate definition risk
plan_bridge_items: Archaeology: records/endpoints/[name].ts 1:1 with flow.md § Referenced Endpoints — endpoint list no longer by memory
Planning: contract-schemas/endpoints/[name].md as SSOT for naming and Transformer; shared/ consolidates cross-endpoint entities
Code directory slug aligns with contract-schemas filename (e.g. get-bus-list), one endpoint per folder
First vertical slice defines golden sample (incl. Zod + test); subsequent endpoints copy structure, no reinventing
Role 2 must check contract-schemas/shared/ before starting; duplicate entity definitions forbidden
s9.bff_note: BFF discussion evaluated location search, 7k-record cache, etc. — most can be done with frontend IndexedDB + local filter; only a few geographic computation scenarios need backend. Default path remains client-side transformer, not a separate BFF service.
